rationale: The cited paper is highly topically aligned with the citing study: both examine competitive effects of advertising on sales outcomes (citing: “advertising spillovers” and advertiser returns; cited: “competitive advertising interference” attenuating advertising elasticity). The citing note explicitly says it “extends prior work on advertising spillovers and ad response,” and this 2008 JMRK paper is a direct candidate for that prior work in the same problem space. With an exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.45.2.211), this is not an obvious incidental mention and should be retained for full relation assessment.
